fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 76 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: (clamp == True) == (y__1 != min(max(y__1, min(y0, y1)), max(y0, y1)) or True)

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch that performs clamping should be entered, not skipped; the condition `if not clamp` is backwards.
The then-branch of the if-statement should only execute when clamp is False (i.e., clamping is disabled), confirming the condition is inverted.

spec.expr: not clamp and min(y0, y1) <= y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After clamping, y__2 should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)], and this block should only run when clamp is False.

spec.expr: (clamp and y__3 == min(max(y__1, min(y0, y1)), max(y0, y1))) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: Before y__3 is used, it should equal the clamped value when clamp=True and the raw interpolated value when clamp=False.
